3.speaker: Crab
3.text: What sorts of things go on in that place?

choices.text: People speak what they believe is true.

4_1.speaker: Crab
4_1.text: Words spoken that go to no-one...

4_2.speaker: Crab
4_2.text: To speak what you believe, as if saying it makes it more real.

5.speaker: Crab
5.text: Always strange things going on in their minds. I never quite understood it.

6.speaker: Crab
6.text: The truth seems to be something that your people always strive to find out.

7.speaker: You
7.text: Maybe it's because there's lot of things that aren't the real truth.

7.1.speaker: You
7.1.text: They say history is written by the victors.

8.speaker: Crab
8.text: Like the people that fight in that battle? Only those who survive get to tell their truths?

10.speaker: Crab
10.text: What about those who've fallen? Are they forgotten? Are their stories untold?

12.speaker: Crab
12.text: And in that... 'church.' They are the victors too? What makes them different?

choices.text: Prayers aren't always heard by everyone.

13_1_1.speaker: You
13_1_1.text: But written records aren't the only proof of existence.

13_1_2.speaker: You
13_1_2.text: Everything leaves a drag path. Whether it's faintly on dirt or etched in stone.

13_2_1.speaker: You
13_2_1.text: Prayers aren't always heard by everyone.

13_2_2.speaker: You
13_2_2.text: Sometimes they aren't even spoken out loud.

13_2_3.speaker: You
13_2_3.text: But they still are meaningful to those who do it.

14.speaker: You
14.text: A life doesn't need to be recorded to be lived.

18.speaker: Crab
18.text: Let's keep going. I'd like to know more of your thoughts.
